does: This paper identifies and addresses training instabilities when applying reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to distillation-trained reasoning models. The authors discover two key failure modes: Sequence Length Collapse, where generated text length drops sharply during early RLHF training, and the Reward Hockey Stick Curve, showing severe initial reward degradation followed by slow recovery. They propose Balanced Actor Initialization (BAI), a two-stage weighted model merging technique that first combines instruction-following and distillation-based reasoning models, then merges the result with the pretrained base model to create a stable initialization point. This approach prevents catastrophic performance drops and enables effective RLHF training of complex reasoning models while maintaining both alignment and reasoning capabilities.
connects: This work bridges two major paradigms in LLM development: instruction tuning with RLHF for alignment (following methods like InstructGPT and subsequent scaling studies) and distillation-based reasoning fine-tuning (building on chain-of-thought distillation approaches). The paper responds to a practical gap where combining these paradigms directly leads to training failure. It connects to broader research on training stability in RL for language models, model merging techniques like TIES-Merging and task arithmetic that enable combining multiple specialized models, and studies of RLHF failure modes and initialization sensitivity. The work also relates to ongoing research on scaling reasoning capabilities and maintaining performance across multiple objectives simultaneously.
enables: This work enables practical deployment of complex reasoning models with RLHF-based alignment, opening pathways for developing models that simultaneously exhibit strong reasoning, instruction-following, and human-aligned behavior. The stable training framework could facilitate research into multi-objective fine-tuning of large language models and inform broader understanding of when and how to combine different training paradigms. The findings about sequence length collapse and reward curve dynamics establish diagnostic tools for detecting similar instabilities in other multi-stage training procedures. Future work can build on BAI to develop more sophisticated initialization strategies, extend the approach to other combinations of training objectives, and investigate whether similar merging-based stabilization helps with other challenging composite training scenarios in language model development.
